sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citation occurrences resolve to identifiable pages.

figures_match_sources.note: The pages support broad practitioner AI use and agency multiple ranges, but not the exact score inputs for NAICS 541613. Firm-level material adoption, the historical analog, owner-age scope, consolidator count, exact target/platform multiples and revenue-weighted billing shares all fail contextual support.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: All judgment inputs are bounded and rationalized, but 47.27% of wage share is assumed to be a creative/content-heavy tail at 0.55 automatable without a six-digit occupational allocation. t50_years=2 treats individual marketers' any-use rate as firm-level material adoption while simultaneously saying activity share will not reach 50% for two to three years. pi_dist/pi_moat depend on uncited fee-deflation and bypass claims.

rubric_consistent.note: Identity and deterministic dataset inputs match, and formulas follow the frozen rubric. However, broad advertising-agency ranges are imported into marketing consulting despite the prompt's boundary warning, M inputs are profile estimates rather than actual paid exact-industry multiples, and the succession bonus uses a general owner/advisor shortage. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S, V is reviewed and is not independently disqualifying because Stage 4 found the deltas within tolerance.

cross_checks_honest.note: The cross-checks are explicit but overstate the evidence. The terminal check asserts agency-revenue decline, job losses and consultant-quality client substitution without auditable citations in the record and conflates advertising agencies with 541613. The pricing check presents a 60%/30%/10% mix and industry-wide fee deflation that the cited generic pricing page does not establish.

source_audits.note: The third-party guest post describes several agency investments and tuck-ins, but contains no 42.4% first-half-2025 PE share and does not identify eight qualifying PE-backed platforms actively buying NAICS 541613 marketing consultancies.

The guide gives agency profile ranges of 2-4x EBITDA for project-only generalists and 4-6x for mixed-retainer firms. It does not report a 4.0x median actually paid for small marketing-consulting firms or show that the scored 541613 target mix matches an agency tier.

HubSpot reports 66% of more than 1,000 global marketing and advertising professionals use AI in their roles. It does not show 74% U.S. adoption, does not measure firms, and does not establish 65% material deployment across automatable functions by U.S. marketing consultancies.

The guide gives 6-9x for retainer-heavy agencies and 7-12x for specialty agencies, with an 8-12x holding-company add-on illustration. It does not contain the quoted 8.1-10.7x $5-10 million EBITDA tier or establish 9.0x for $10 million-plus EBITDA NAICS 541613 platforms.

The 2013 HBR article addresses broad consumer technology diffusion. It does not mention marketing automation or support a 2010-2018 path to 50% mid-market adoption, nor the claimed 5-8 year marketing-tool interval.

The secondary page supports all-industry ABS age bins and a general transition-advisor shortage; interpolation gives about 37.5% age 60-plus. It provides no marketing-consulting owner-age or industry-specific succession-shortage evidence, yet the value is marked MED and drives an OW bonus.

The page defines hourly, fixed-fee, retainer, project and outcome-based consulting models. It does not rank project and retainer as dominant for marketing consulting or quantify a 60% project, 30% retainer, 10% value/performance mix.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://blog.hubspot.com/marketing/state-of-ai-report), individual global practitioner use is treated as 65% material firm adoption in U.S. 541613. Supply a firm-level material-use measure or a transparent fallback chain that distinguishes any tool use from production-scale deployment.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://hbr.org/2013/11/the-pace-of-technology-adoption-is-speeding-up), the asserted 5-8 year marketing-automation analog is absent. Re-run t50_years from documented marketing-technology adoption endpoints and the corrected current-adoption input.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[10], nearly half the wage bill is asserted to be dominated by writers/designers/content roles and assigned a 0.55 fraction without six-digit occupational evidence. Build a supportable tail composition and apply separate task fractions before recomputing the input.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.investorideas.com/news/2026/main/01152-private-equity-buying-marketing-agencies-2026-ai-ma.asp), a narrative guest post does not support eight active marketing-consulting consolidators. Name each PE-backed platform and qualifying recent 541613 add-on, excluding pure advertising-agency deals.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/marketing-agency-business-valuation/), broad agency profile ranges are converted to 4.0x and 9.0x exact-industry score inputs without transaction medians or boundary analysis. Supply normalized paid multiples for marketing-consulting targets and $10 million-plus EBITDA platforms.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://nmsconsulting.com/consulting-fees-and-pricing-in-2026/), generic model definitions are converted into unsupported 60%/30%/10% revenue shares that drive pi_dist. Supply representative revenue-weighted billing evidence and direct evidence of fee compression versus engagement-volume loss.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/boomer-business-succession-wave-report-2024-2030/), all-industry age and transition-advisor data is treated as MED 541613 evidence and triggers the succession bonus. Use a marketing/professional-services proxy with candid quality and an applicable shortage finding.

At cross_checks.terminal_value.justification and inputs.pi_moat.rationale, uncited advertising-agency revenue/job claims are used to classify marketing consulting as MELTING. Supply auditable 541613-comparable demand, fee, insourcing and engagement-volume evidence and separate consulting from advertising/media execution before reassessing the terminal class.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S, V
